title: Security in Node.js Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of Helmet security headers, rate limiting (brute force prevention), bcrypt password hashing, and preventing data injection.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily role of 'Security' in a Node.js application?
options: Protecting user data and server integrity by preventing common attacks like injection, brute force, and data leaks.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.

questions.question: Which popular Express middleware set several security-related HTTP headers to make your application harder to exploit?
Which technique restricts the number of requests a single IP address can make to protect the server from hammering?
Which security best practice should you ALWAYS follow when storing user passwords as data in your database?
options: Store them in plain text for easy access.
Securely hash and salt them using a library like bcrypt.

questions.question: Does using a modern ORM like Prisma or Sequelize help preventing SQL and NoSQL Injection attacks?
options: Yes, as they automatically use parameterized queries to handle untrusted user data safely.
